data.title: BP expands board with veteran Dave Hager as non-executive director
data.description:
• BP (NYSE:BP) appointed David Hager—a veteran of the U.S. oil and gas industry, as a non-executive director effective June 2, 2025.
  • Hager has over 40 years of experience in the sector and served as CEO of Devon Energy from 2015 to 2021 and as executive chair from 2021 to 2023.
  • The appointment comes at a time when BP (NYSE:BP) is shifting its strategy to increase hydrocarbon production, aiming to more than double its oil and gas output in the U.S. by the end of the decade. 
  • The move aligns with the company's broader strategic reset, which includes cutting spending on low-carbon investments and focusing more on oil and gas production.
  • "On behalf of the board, I am delighted to welcome Dave to bp. He brings deep-rooted knowledge of the US upstream oil and gas industry. His strategic focus combined with his experience in driving operational results and capital efficiency will be an asset to the board." said Helge Lund, chair of BP.

data.title: Primary Hydrogen announces non-brokered private placement
data.description:
• Primary Hydrogen (HNATF) intends to complete a non-brokered private placement for gross proceeds of up to C$2M. 
  • The placement consists of two parts: up to ~3.13M flow-through units at $0.48 per unit (the FT unit) and up to 1.25M non-flow through units at $0.40 per unit.
  • The proceeds from the sale of the Non-FT Units are intended to be used general working capital and corporate purposes.
  • The proceeds from the sale of the Non-FT Units are intended to be used general working capital and corporate purposes.

data.title: UP Fintech Non-GAAP EPS of $0.20, revenue of $122.6M beats by $11M
data.description:
• UP Fintech press release (NASDAQ:TIGR): Q1 Non-GAAP EPADS of $0.20.
  • Revenue of $122.6M (+55.3% Y/Y) beats by $11M.
  • Total net revenues were $107.6 million, an increase of 67.7% year-over-year and an increase of 0.2% quarter-over-quarter.
  • Total account balance increased 39.5% year-over-year to US$45.9 billion.
  • Total margin financing and securities lending balance increased 89.4% year-over-year to $5.2 billion.
  • Total number of customers with deposit increased 23.5% year-over-year to 1,152,900.
  • As of March 31, 2025, the company's cash and cash equivalents, term deposits and long-term deposits were $406.4 million, compared to $396 million as of December 31, 2024.
  • "In the first quarter, we added 60,900 new customers with deposits, already achieving 40% of our yearly guidance of 150,000 new customers with deposits for 2025, and bringing our total number of customers with deposits at the end of the first quarter to 1,152,900, a 23.5% increase compared to the same quarter last year."

data.title: European markets muted, Germany's inflation in focus
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.57% to 8,767.

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.59% to 24,073, Germany's retail sales rise 2.3% Y/Y in April.

France (CAC:IND) +0.16% to 7,792. 

Spain's inflation drops 1.9% Y/Y in May.

The Czech Republic’s gross domestic product advanced by 0.8% Q/Q in Q1.

Finland’s economy stalled Q/Q in the first three months of 2025.

The Swedish economy shrank by 0.2% on a quarterly basis.

The Turkish economy expanded 2% Y/Y in Q1.

Hungary’s annual producer inflation edged up to 7.9% in April.

Producer prices in Austria fell by 0.2% Y/Y in April.

The KOF Economic Barometer in Switzerland rose to 98.5 in May.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) gained 0.32% to 549.65, with uncertainty around the U.S. trade war likely to persist. Meanwhile, US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent noted that trade negotiations with China were “a bit stalled”. The euro edged slightly lower, hovering near the $1.13 mark and steady compared to the previous month's level. 

Coming up in the session: Germany's inflation numbers expected later in the day.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield remains unchnaged at 4.42%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down less than 1 basis point to 2.51%.

UK's 10-year yield was up less than 1 basis point at 4.66%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Trillium Acquisition scraps planned business combination
data.description:
Trillium Acquisition Corp. (TCK.P:CA) announced the termination of its previously disclosed business combination agreement with Bankview 1827 Investment Corp., Capitol Hill 1426 Investment Corp., and Sunalta 1509 Development Corp. (the "Vendors") and their affiliates.

The agreement, originally signed May 17, 2024, and amended on October 24, 2024, and March 30, 2025, will not move forward.

data.title: Marvell Technology in charts: Q1 data center revenue +76.5%, enterprise networking +16%
Japan's Nomura stays committed to growth in U.S. despite turmoil
data.description:
Nomura (NYSE:NMR) reaffirmed commitment to expanding its business operations in the U.S., despite recent market turbulence due to global tariff disputes.

"Currently, market volatility is increasing due to global tariff negotiations, and the U.S. can be said to be the epicenter of all this,” CEO Kentaro Okuda said, emphasizing that, despite being at the epicenter of recent market volatility, the U.S. remains central to Nomura’s global strategy.

The U.S. accounted for 14% of the Japanese investment banker's income before taxes in the fiscal year ending March 2025, underscoring its importance to the bank’s global earnings.

In April 2025, Nomura (NYSE:NMR) announced its largest-ever acquisition with $1.8 billion purchase of the U.S. and European public asset management businesses from Australia’s Macquarie Group. This move is intended to strengthen the company's position in both the U.S. and European markets.

The firm highlighted “significant opportunities” and set ambitious profit targets for its newly established banking division.

data.title: Ulta Beauty in charts: Sales growth picks up Y/Y, records 6 new store openings in Q1
Bessent: U.S.-China trade talks 'a bit' stalled, Trump and Xi may need to weigh in
data.description:
U.S. trade talks with China are "a bit stalled", Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told Fox News, adding that the countries' presidents Donald Trump and Xi Jinping will need to speak to each other to reach a deal.

"Given the magnitude of the talks, given the complexity, this is going to require both leaders to weigh in with each other," Bessent said. "They have a very good relationship. And I am confident that the Chinese will come to the table when Trump makes his preferences known."

Bessent added that the two countries will engage in more negotiations in the next few weeks. "And I believe we may, at some point, have a call between the president and party chair Xi."

The U.S. and China reached a temporary truce earlier this month, with both countries agreeing to roll back tariffs hikes till mid-August. Even so, the U.S. has moved ahead with tech restrictions on China, while Beijing has yet to significantly ease curbs on critical mineral exports.

data.title: New World seeks more banks to join up to $2 billion loan - report
data.description:
New World Development (OTCPK:NDVLY) is reportedly looking for more banks to join an up to HK$15.6 billion ($2 billion) loan backed by its key asset, Victoria Dockside, as the cash-strapped company tries to shore up its finances.

Deustche Bank, which is arranging the deal, sent invitations this week to some banks that aren’t already lenders to the developer, Bloomberg reported.

The base size of the three-year facility is HK$4 billion (S$657.8 million). Proposed terms that the company sent to other banks in March didn’t specify a base loan size, the report said.

Some of New World’s existing lenders have since decided not to participate in the transaction because they are separately engaged in an HK$87.5 billion refinancing exercise by the company, the report added.

The financiers are reportedly wary of increasing their exposure to the company, and the Banks must finalise their commitments to the new loan by July 11.
